steps.text: For a linear function \(f(x) = mx\) passing through the origin, the slope \(m\) equals \(f(x)/x\) for any data point. Use the first row of the table: \(x = 2{,}520\) sq ft, \(f(x) = 4{,}536\) gallons. Compute: \(4536 \div 2520 = 1.8\).
Verify with the other rows: \(6804 \div 3780 = 1.8\) and \(9072 \div 5040 = 1.8\). The ratio is constant, confirming the model is \(f(x) = 1.8x\).

Evaluate every choice:
  • A: \(f(x)=0.6x\) — Gives \(0.6 \times 2520 = 1512\), not 4536. Eliminated.
  • B: \(f(x)=1.8x\) — Gives \(1.8 \times 2520 = 4536\). Matches all rows. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: \(f(x)=2268x\) — Gives an astronomically large value for any reasonable roof area. Eliminated.
  • D: \(f(x)=4536x\) — Same issue; value is far too large. Eliminated.
